# Handoffs (Multi-Agent Orchestration)

One agent can only do so much. A core design pattern in modern AI systems is the **Triage and Handoff** pattern, where a router agent directs queries to specialized agents.

## Implementing a Handoff

In the `openai-agents` Python SDK, you can return another `Agent` directly from a tool to initiate a handoff!

```python
from openai_agents import Agent, Runner, tool
import asyncio

# The specialized agent
support_agent = Agent(
    name="SupportSpecialist",
    instructions="You help users with billing issues.",
    model="gpt-4o-mini"
)

# Tool that hands off to the specialist
@tool
async def handoff_to_support() -> Agent:
    """Call this tool to transfer the user to the billing support specialist."""
    return support_agent

# The Router / Triage agent
triage_agent = Agent(
    name="TriageAgent",
    instructions="Determine what the user needs. If it's a billing issue, transfer them to support.",
    tools=[handoff_to_support],
    model="gpt-4o-mini"
)

async def main():
    result = await Runner.run(
        triage_agent, 
        input="I need a refund for my last invoice."
    )
    
    # The SDK automatically invokes the handoff and runs the SupportSpecialist!
    print(f"Final Agent that answered: {result.agent.name}")
    print(f"Response: {result.final_output}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())
```

This pattern drastically reduces the cognitive load on a single model and improves determinism.
